title: Epic: multi-project-readiness — solidify jjf across N projects and machines
body:
# Status

**Done as of 2026-06-25.** All 11 children closed; 10-project
multi-machine adoption is unblocked.

# Goal

`jjf` should hold up as the issue tracker across ~10 projects
with multiple collaborators, multiple machines per person, and
agent-driven automation gated on exit codes. Today the
single-repo experience is solid, but multi-machine adoption
is blocked by three CRITICAL bugs (F-001 / F-002 / F-003) that
share a root cause around `refs/jjf/meta/format-version`.

End state: a brand-new collaborator runs the documented clone
recipe, sees every issue on first `jjf ls`, and every `jjf
pull` / `jjf push` exits 0 cleanly.

# Approach

The work splits into three categories:

1. **Sync fidelity (criticals).** Fix the meta-ref handling so
   clone-then-pull-then-push round-trips exit 0. Likely one or
   two small commits in the storage + push/pull layers.

2. **Graph integrity (highs).** Reject dep cycles at write time;
   add a `jjf doctor` (or `jjf dep ls`) verb so an admin can
   audit graph and ref health.

3. **Sharp edges (mediums + docs).** Slug-recycle policy,
   corrupt-ref reporting, error-kind taxonomy, CLAUDE.md /
   quickstart updates pulled out of the QA findings.

# Provenance

Findings sourced from an adversarial QA pass on 2026-06-24
covering quickstart literal-follow, multi-clone sync, concurrent
writers, JSON contract, dep graph, storage corruption, roadmap
singleton, and memory/edge-case probing. Full report:
`scratchpad/qa/findings.md` (preserved out of tree).

# Child inventory

Discover via `jjf ls --label epic:multi-project-readiness
--status all`.
